summary: Iteration 5 latency-sensitive test. Backend GET/DELETE APIs healthy. Frontend: piano labels on ALL keys (including non-C) verified, delete button always visible + working with 'Song removed' toast, play-original-button correctly hidden for demo songs, hidden original-audio element present, play/pause/stop/speed regression intact, zero console errors.
passed: Piano key labels visible on 6/6 non-C keys (D4, F#4, G4, A4, A#4, E5) plus A0 & C8 boundaries
Delete button always visible (opacity=1, 26x26) — no hover required
Clicking delete removes song from userSongs (6 -> 5) and shows 'Song removed' toast
hasOriginal=false for demo song → play-original-button NOT rendered (correct)
Hidden <audio data-testid='original-audio'> element present in DOM
Regression: Play → Pause button swap, Stop, speed slider all functional
Zero console errors on load and interaction
notes: WAV upload of /tmp/test_piano_scale.wav to verify cleaner MIDI extraction (piano range clamp, polyphony <=8, note count 4-16) and to observe play-original-button appearing was NOT executed in Playwright because @spotify/basic-pitch model download + inference is heavy/slow in headless mode. Code review of /app/frontend/src/lib/audioToMidi.js confirms refineNotes performs (a) PIANO_MIN=21/PIANO_MAX=108 clamp, (b) 40ms sweep polyphony reduction keeping top maxPolyphony=8 by velocity, (c) same-pitch merge with 30ms gap. TransportControls conditionally renders play-original-button on hasOriginal prop tied to audioUrls[currentSong.id] populated only for isAudio uploads. Recommend full-testing agent perform live WAV upload to confirm empirically.
